# Deck of Many Allies - Rules (en-us)

## Basic Rules

### Introduction

These rules explain how to use the **Deck of Many Allies** at a Daggerheart table.

Allies represent secondary characters who fight, assist, and interact with the party during a scene.

---

### Introducing an Ally

When an ally enters play, give their card to a player.

That player becomes responsible for:

- controlling the ally’s positioning  
- deciding when to activate their abilities  
- managing the tokens on the card  

An ally always acts alongside the player who controls them.

---

### Spotlight

Allies share the **spotlight** of the player who controls them.

Whenever that player **has the spotlight**, the ally is also considered to have the spotlight.

This allows the player to:

- move the ally  
- activate the ally’s abilities  
- interact with the battlefield through them

---

### Positioning and Movement

An ally is represented by a miniature on the battle map, just like any character.

When the player who controls the ally **has the spotlight**, they can move the ally to a point within **Close** range as part of their actions.

Allies follow the same rules for range and positioning as player characters.

---

### Tokens

Most allies use **tokens** to activate their abilities.

Tokens can be represented by:

- dice  
- counters  
- coins  
- any simple object available at the table  

An ally card begins each **scene** with **0 tokens**, unless an ability states otherwise.

At the end of each **session**, remove all tokens from ally cards.

---

### Activating Abilities

A player can activate an ally’s abilities **whenever they have the spotlight**, unless the ability specifies a particular timing.

If an ability requires tokens, they must be removed from the card when the effect is activated.

---

### Token Limit

An ally card can have a maximum of **6 tokens** at a time.

If an effect would add tokens beyond this limit, ignore the excess.

Some cards may modify this limit. When this happens, the card will state the new rule.

---

### Damage and Stress

By default in Daggerheart games, allies do not have their own stat blocks, Hit Points, or Stress. They represent narrative and mechanical support for the player characters.

Even so, allies are not immune to danger. They can be seriously harmed or even die, depending on the fiction of the scene.

It is up to the GM to decide:

- how adversaries interact with allies  
- when an ally is put at risk  
- what the narrative consequences of these events are

---

## Optional Rules

### Hit Points and Stress

If you wish to treat allies as full characters, you can assign them Hit Points and Stress.

Suggested values:

- **Hit Points:** 3 to 5  
- **Stress:** 1 to 3

---

### Damage Thresholds

Use the table below to determine damage thresholds based on the campaign tier:

| Tier | Major Damage | Severe Damage |
|------|--------------|---------------|
| 1st  | 7            | 12            |
| 2nd  | 10           | 20            |
| 3rd  | 20           | 32            |
| 4th  | 25           | 40            |

---

## Keywords

### Channel

> Whenever this ally gains the spotlight, place 1 token on this card.

Allies who Channel build power gradually while they remain in the spotlight. Each moment in focus increases their available energy.

---

### Foresee

> Whenever an ally uses an Experience, place 1 token on this card.

Allies with Foresee perceive patterns, signs, and coincidences. When someone uses their experience to influence fate, these allies grow stronger.

---

### Inspired

> Whenever an ally rolls with Hope on their Duality Dice, place 1 token on this card.

Inspired allies thrive when fortune favors the group. Each roll with Hope fuels their momentum, allowing them to use their abilities more often.

---

### Opportunist

> Whenever an ally fails an attack roll, place 1 token on this card.

Opportunist turn their allies’ missteps into openings. When an ally’s attack fails, they seize the moment to strike.

---

### Prepared

> This ally begins with a fixed number of tokens shown on the card and cannot gain additional tokens until the next rest.

Prepared allies enter the scene with resources already at hand. These tokens represent planning, supplies, or energy gathered before the encounter.

---

### Pressured

> Whenever an ally rolls with Fear on their Duality Dice, place 1 token on this card.

Pressured allies thrive when the situation turns dangerous. Moments of tension and risk awaken their survival instincts.

---

### Track

> Whenever an ally deals damage to an adversary, place 1 token on this card.

Allies with Track thrive in the heat of battle. Each successful strike brings their quarry closer.

---

### Triumph

> Whenever an adversary is defeated, place 1 token on this card.

Allies with Triumph grow stronger as the battle turns in their favor. Each defeated adversary builds their momentum.

---

### Witness

> Whenever an ally marks the amount of Hit Points shown on this card or more, place 1 token on this card.

Allies with Witness draw strength from the suffering and struggle of their companions. The higher the threshold, the more damage must be marked to trigger this ability.
